record_identity.title: Microsoft Teams adds meeting-level controls for AI participation

summary: Microsoft reportedly began rolling out Teams controls allowing licensed meeting organizers and presenters to switch Meeting AI features—including Copilot, Facilitator, and Intelligent Recap—on or off during live meetings. Administrator policy still determines whether the controls and features are available. The change is a positive repair precedent for situational AI activation and human override, but it does not by itself establish individual participant consent for transcription, inference, summarization, memory, or downstream reuse.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Multi-party AI participation requires more than organization-level deployment approval. Meeting-level activation and deactivation controls are a meaningful governance improvement because they make AI presence situational rather than permanently assumed and provide a human override during a shared interaction. However, organizer or presenter authority is not equivalent to each participant's informed consent. The observation therefore supports layered control across administrator authorization, host activation, participant notice, individual rights, processing scope, retention, and downstream use.

source_records.source_title: Microsoft Teams just made it easier to shut down Copilot and other AI tools
source_records.model_or_algorithm: Microsoft Copilot, Facilitator, and Intelligent Recap
source_records.deployment_context: Microsoft Teams meetings across desktop, web, and mobile where Meeting AI features are enabled by organizational administrators.
source_records.source_context: Windows Central reported an upcoming or rolling Microsoft Teams control allowing licensed meeting organizers to disable all Meeting AI features together or manage Copilot, Facilitator, and Intelligent Recap individually. The report states that administrator policy remains controlling and that the change applies across Teams platforms.
source_records.relevance_note: Evidence of a platform-level human override and situational activation control for AI participation in multi-party meetings.

source_records.source_title: Microsoft makes major AI U-turn following user revolt — will let Teams users turn off Copilot, Facilitator and Recap
source_records.model_or_algorithm: Microsoft Copilot, Facilitator, and Intelligent Recap
source_records.deployment_context: Live Microsoft Teams meetings with licensed organizers or presenters and administrator-enabled Meeting AI functionality.
source_records.source_context: TechRadar reported that Microsoft was adding an in-meeting toggle for licensed organizers and presenters to activate or deactivate Meeting AI features during live meetings, with rollout beginning in early July 2026 and organizational administrator settings remaining relevant.
source_records.relevance_note: Corroborates the reported meeting-level AI control and highlights the continuing distinction between administrator, organizer, presenter, and participant authority.

system_context.system_type: enterprise collaboration platform with embedded meeting AI
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: Microsoft Teams Meeting AI, including Copilot, Facilitator, and Intelligent Recap
system_context.model_or_product: Microsoft Teams with Meeting AI features
system_context.interaction_mode: multi-party voice, video, transcription, facilitation, summarization, and meeting recap
system_context.deployment_context: Enterprise, public-sector, educational, and organizational meetings where AI features may observe, process, summarize, or facilitate participant interaction.
system_context.affected_population: Teams meeting participants and organizations using Meeting AI features

jurisdictional_context.sector: enterprise collaboration / workplace AI / meeting platforms

possible_taxonomy_mapping.candidate_failure_description: A multi-party environment activates AI observation, facilitation, transcription, summarization, memory, or inference without sufficiently layered authorization, notice, override, and participant control.
possible_taxonomy_mapping.classification_limit: This observation records a positive platform control rather than a confirmed failure. Public reporting does not establish the exact participant notice, consent, data-retention, transcript, inference, memory, or downstream-use behaviour of every Teams configuration.

related_or_similar_instruments: CAM-EQ2026-RELATION-007-PLATINUM — Appendix F: Polyadic Relational Governance Doctrine
CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
cam_internal.governance_layer: multi-party AI participation / situational activation / human override / participant notice and consent / meeting-data governance
routing_note: Record the Teams change as a positive repair precedent, not as proof that Microsoft has fully resolved participant-consent or meeting-data governance.

Distinguish organizational administrator authorization, organizer or presenter activation, participant notice, individual consent or objection, processing scope, retention, memory, and downstream reuse.

FM-0002 and PATCH-0008 are adjacent because they govern shared synthetic participation and human-floor authority, although the present observation concerns AI activation and processing in a meeting rather than competing synthetic speakers.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: generated observation and master registry indexes require rebuild

observation_status.recurrence_status: platform change reported across two technology publications
observation_status.resolution_status: meeting-level override reported; participant-level rights and processing details remain configuration-dependent or unclear
observation_status.observed_pattern: Microsoft is adding a live-meeting control that makes embedded AI features situationally defeasible by licensed organizers or presenters rather than always active when administratively enabled.
observation_status.repair_linkage: The change provides an external implementation precedent relevant to CAM's human-floor sovereignty, shared-participation governance, transparency, and override requirements.
observation_status.monitoring_note: Confirm the final rollout documentation and assess whether participants receive meaningful notice and controls over transcription, inference, summarization, retention, memory, and reuse.

next_action: Preserve this as a positive repair precedent, verify final Microsoft documentation when publicly available, and compare the implemented controls against layered CAM requirements for administrator authorization, host activation, participant notice, individual consent or objection, processing scope, retention, memory, and downstream reuse. Do not treat organizer-level control as equivalent to participant-level consent.
